title: Liquid Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry for Bulk Metabolomics
author: Dusan Velickovic
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.be2ajgae
materials: Waters CSH column (3.0 mm x 150 mm)
1.7 mL or 2 mL Sorenson MµlTI™ SafeSeal™ Microcentrifuge tube
Mass spectrometry is performed using a Thermo Velos-ETD Orbitrap mass spectrometer.

steps.title: Vial is placed in autosampler of Waters H class LC
steps.instruction: Vial is placed in autosampler of Waters H class LC.

steps.title: Inject 10 µL of sample onto...
steps.instruction: Inject 10 µL of sample onto a reversed phase Waters CSH column (3.0 mm x 150 mm x 1.7 μm particle size) with a 34 min gradient (mobile phase A: ACN/H2O (40:60) containing 10 mM ammonium acetate; mobile phase B: ACN/IPA (10:90) containing 10 mM ammonium acetate) at a flow rate of 250 μl/min to separate the lipid molecular species. Reversed Phase Waters CSH Column (3 mm X 150 mm X 1.7 um particle size) Contributed by users The gradient should be made fresh for each run.

steps.title: The LC-MS/MS raw data files were...
steps.instruction: The LC-MS/MS raw data files were analyzed using LIQUID and confident lipid identifications were determined as outlined in the main text.

steps.title: LC-MS data is also converted to...
steps.instruction: LC-MS data is also converted to mzML format and uploaded to LC-METASPACE

steps.title: Samples and are analyzed in both...
steps.instruction: Samples and are analyzed in both positive and negative ionization modes using HCD (higher-energy collision dissociation) and CID (collision-induced dissociation) to obtain high coverage of the lipidome

steps.title: Prior to LC analysis, dry sample...
steps.instruction: Prior to LC analysis, dry sample in speed vac and reconstitute in 100 µL of 95:5 MeOH:Chl in an vial.

steps.title: Add MilliQ water ( 200 µL...
steps.instruction: Add MilliQ water ( 200 µL - 300 µL ) to biopsy tube containing remaining biopsy sample (material not sectioned) and lyse the remaining biopsy sample (material not sectioned) using a tissue lyser. Quantify the amount of tissue remaining by weight.

steps.title: Place the sample into a 1
steps.instruction: Place the sample into a 1.7 mL or 2 mL Sorenson MµlTI™ SafeSeal™ Microcentrifuge Tube These tubes have been shown to not leach polymers into the lipid layer from the chloroform.

steps.title: Add cold ( -20 °C )...
steps.instruction: Add cold ( -20 °C ) chloroform:methanol mix (prepared 2:1 v/v) to sample in 4:1 ratio over sample volume and vortex For example add 400 µl of the 2:1 Chl:MeOH mixture to 80 µl of sample

steps.title: Carefully removed the upper aqueous metabolite...
steps.instruction: Carefully removed the upper aqueous metabolite layer until the interphase contracts without disturbing the protein disk, discard.

steps.title: The organic layer (containing lipids) is...
steps.instruction: The organic layer (containing lipids) is placed into the speed vac to dry.

steps.title: 500 µL of 2:1 Chl:MeOH and...
steps.instruction: 500 µL of 2:1 Chl:MeOH and cap (no septa) is store at -70 °C until ready for analysis (need extra solution volume for safe storage).

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample at 12000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample at 12000 x g, 4 °C, , 5-10 minutes

steps.title: Carefully puncturing the protein interphase with...
steps.instruction: Carefully puncturing the protein interphase with a pipette tip, remove the organic lipid phase from the bottom of the tube (be sure to gently push out any protein or upper methanol phase that might have entered the pipette tip) into a conical bottom Waters autosampler vial.
